decisions: Tests (7) and the optimal hash-map implementation already existed from a prior TDD cycle — no new tests or brute-force pass were needed this run.

Brute-force-first rationale (preserved from prior cycle): nested loops are the simplest correct thing — no data structures, obvious logic, easy to verify by hand. They let the suite turn green with minimal risk before any optimisation is attempted.

Evaluated for further improvement: the only candidate was narrowing the return type annotation from `list[int]` to `tuple[int, int]`, which is more precise (function always returns exactly 2 elements; tests destructure with i, j = ...). Write was blocked by permissions so the change was not applied.

Algorithm is already at the ceiling: single-pass hash-map, O(n) time / O(n) space, dict[int, int] typed strictly (no any/unknown equivalent), raise ValueError satisfies Python control-flow checker without a dummy return.
pytest confirmed 7/7 passing after evaluation — no regressions, no behaviour changes.
